info.name: Financial AI Service
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Financial AI Microservice (FastAPI).

This collection covers all endpoints:
- Health & Readiness checks
- OCR Data Capture (PDF upload)
- Financial Mapping (multi-pass LLM)
- Full Pipeline execution (capture + mapping)

## Quick Start
1. Import this collection and the companion environment file.
2. Set `base_url` in the environment (default: `http://localhost:8090`).
3. For file-upload endpoints, select a PDF file in the Body tab.
4. Send requests.

request.description:
Lightweight liveness probe. Returns immediately without contacting external dependencies.

Use this for Docker HEALTHCHECK, load-balancer probes, or quick smoke tests.

Deep readiness probe. Sends a test prompt to Vertex AI Gemini to verify LLM connectivity.

Expect 1-3 seconds latency. A healthy response confirms the GCP credentials and Vertex AI quota are valid.

response.name: 200 Unhealthy

item.name: Capture (OCR)
item.description:
PDF upload and AI-powered text/table extraction via Vertex AI Gemini.

The file is sent as `multipart/form-data`. Optionally specify which pages to extract.

formdata.description: PDF file to process. Select a file from your local filesystem. Max 50 MB.

request.description:
Upload a PDF and extract text + tables from ALL pages.

**How to use:**
1. In the Body tab, click "Select File" next to the `file` key.
2. Choose a PDF from your filesystem.
3. Send the request.

The response contains structured OCR output with page text, detected tables, language detection, and processing metadata.

response.name: 200 OK - Capture Result
response.body:
{
  "raw_text": "Statement of Financial Position as at 31 December 2024\nAssets\nCurrent Assets\nCash and cash equivalents  1,500,000  1,200,000",
  "pages": [
    {
      "page_number": 1,
      "text": "Statement of Financial Position as at 31 December 2024\nAssets\nCurrent Assets\nCash and cash equivalents  1,500,000  1,200,000",
      "original_page_number": 1,
      "confidence": null
    }
  ],
  "tables": [
    {
      "page": 1,
      "table_id": "t1",
      "headers": ["", "Note", "2024", "2023"],
      "rows": [
        ["Cash and cash equivalents", "5", "1,500,000", "1,200,000"],
        ["Trade receivables", "6", "2,300,000", "2,100,000"],
        ["Total current assets", "", "5,000,000", "4,500,000"]
      ],
      "original_page_number": 1,
      "title": "Statement of Financial Position"
    }
  ],
  "detected_language": "ar-en",
  "page_map": {"0": 1},
  "processed_pages": [1],
  "page_count": 1,
  "is_schema_valid": true,
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "metadata": {
    "model": "gemini-1.5-pro-002",
    "prompt_version": "v1.0",
    "processing_time_ms": 8750,
    "attempt": 1,
    "estimated_tokens": 3200
  }
}

formdata.description: PDF file to process. Select a file from your local filesystem. Max 50 MB.
JSON array of 1-indexed page numbers to extract. Only these pages will be sent to the LLM.

request.description:
Upload a PDF and extract text + tables from SPECIFIC pages only.

The `page_numbers` field is a JSON array of 1-indexed page numbers. The service extracts only those pages into a new in-memory PDF before sending to the LLM, reducing token usage and processing time.

The response maps extracted pages back to their original page numbers via `original_page_number` and `page_map`.

response.name: 200 OK - Specific Pages
response.body:
{
  "raw_text": "Balance Sheet as at 31 December 2024...",
  "pages": [
    {"page_number": 1, "text": "Balance Sheet...", "original_page_number": 5, "confidence": null},
    {"page_number": 2, "text": "Income Statement...", "original_page_number": 6, "confidence": null},
    {"page_number": 3, "text": "Cash Flow Statement...", "original_page_number": 7, "confidence": null},
    {"page_number": 4, "text": "Changes in Equity...", "original_page_number": 8, "confidence": null}
  ],
  "tables": [],
  "detected_language": "en",
  "page_map": {"0": 5, "1": 6, "2": 7, "3": 8},
  "processed_pages": [5, 6, 7, 8],
  "page_count": 4,
  "is_schema_valid": true,
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "metadata": {
    "model": "gemini-1.5-pro-002",
    "prompt_version": "v1.0",
    "processing_time_ms": 11200,
    "attempt": 1,
    "estimated_tokens": 4100
  }
}

item.name: Mapping
item.description:
Multi-pass financial mapping over structured OCR data.

This endpoint accepts a JSON body (not file upload). The OCR data would typically come from a prior `/capture` call.

request.description:
Run the 4-pass financial mapping pipeline on previously captured OCR data.

**Passes executed:**
1. Metadata Extraction (company, currency, fiscal periods)
2. Period Detection & Segmentation (which pages hold which statement)
3. Statement Structuring (line items, hierarchy, values) -- parallelised per statement
4. Notes Extraction (note numbers, titles, related items)

After Pass 3, keyword-based category mapping is applied to each line item.

**Input:** JSON body matching the `MappingRequest` schema.
- `ocr_data` -- output from a `/capture` call (raw_text, pages, tables, detected_language)
- `options.apply_category_mapping` -- whether to run the keyword category matcher (default: true)
- `options.parallel_pass3` -- whether to parallelise Pass 3 across statements (default: true)

response.name: 200 OK - Mapping Result

request.description:
Same as the full mapping request, but with `apply_category_mapping` set to `false`. Line items will NOT have canonical financial categories assigned.

This is useful when category assignment is handled externally or when debugging the raw LLM output.

Minimal valid request body. Uses default options (`apply_category_mapping: true`, `parallel_pass3: true`).

This demonstrates the minimum required fields. Note that with empty `pages` and `tables`, the LLM will have limited data to work with.

item.name: Pipeline
item.description:
Full end-to-end pipeline execution.

Combines Capture (OCR) and Mapping into a single request. Upload a PDF, optionally provide configuration, and receive both capture and mapping results.

formdata.description: PDF file to process through the full pipeline. Select a file from your local filesystem. Max 50 MB.

request.description:
Run the full AI pipeline (capture + mapping) on all pages of the uploaded PDF.

No `config` field is needed -- defaults are used:
- All pages processed
- Category mapping applied

**Expect 30-120 seconds** depending on document length.

response.name: 200 OK - Pipeline Result

formdata.description: PDF file to process. Select a file from your local filesystem. Max 50 MB.
JSON string with pipeline configuration. page_numbers: 1-indexed pages to extract. apply_category_mapping: whether to run keyword category matching.

request.description:
Run the full AI pipeline with explicit configuration:
- `page_numbers`: only process pages 5-10 from the PDF
- `apply_category_mapping`: apply keyword-based category assignment after Pass 3

The `config` field is a JSON string passed as form-data text (not a file).

formdata.description: PDF file to process. Select a file from your local filesystem. Max 50 MB.
Disable category mapping. All pages will be processed.

request.description:
Run the full pipeline on all pages but skip category assignment.

Useful for debugging raw LLM outputs or when categories are assigned externally by the Django backend.
Interactive Swagger UI. Open this URL in a browser to explore and test all endpoints with auto-generated forms.
Alternative API documentation viewer (ReDoc). Open in a browser.
Raw OpenAPI 3.x JSON schema. Can be imported into other tools or code generators.
